description: Platform-layer real-time in-app notification delivery rail using SSE, Redis pub/sub fan-out, per-user read state, and 7-day persistence. Domain-agnostic; product services publish via REST.

userStories.title: Project scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Notification Service directory skeleton so that subsequent stories have a working base to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/platform/notification/ with package.json (name: @platform/notification, type: module, Node 24, Fastify 5, Knex 3, pg, @sinclair/typebox 0.34, bullmq, ioredis, jose, vitest 2)
tsconfig.json configured for ESM, strict mode, NodeNext module resolution, output to dist/
src/index.ts starts a Fastify app on port 3006, registers a GET /health route returning { status: 'ok' }, and exports the app for testing
src/config.ts reads DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, JWT_HMAC_SECRET, PORT (default 3006) from process.env; throws on missing required vars
Dockerfile: node:24-alpine, installs deps, copies dist/, CMD ['node', 'dist/index.js']
.env.example lists all required env vars
npm run build (tsc), npm run dev (tsx watch src/index.ts), npm run typecheck (tsc --noEmit), npm run test (vitest run) all present in package.json scripts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Database migrations — notifications schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the platform_notifications schema with correct tables and indexes so that the service can persist and query notifications.
acceptanceCriteria: migrations/001_create_notifications.ts creates table notifications: id uuid PRIMARY KEY, seq bigserial NOT NULL UNIQUE, channel text NOT NULL, title text NOT NULL, body text, payload jsonb, expires_at timestamptz NOT NULL, created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()

migrations/001 creates table notification_reads: user_id uuid NOT NULL, notification_id uuid REFERENCES notifications ON DELETE CASCADE NOT NULL, read_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), PRIMARY KEY (user_id, notification_id)
Index on notifications(channel, created_at DESC, id DESC)
Index on notifications(channel, seq)
Index on notifications(expires_at)
Index on notification_reads(notification_id)
Index on notification_reads(user_id, notification_id) WHERE read_at IS NOT NULL
Migration runs successfully against a local Postgres instance with schema search_path set to platform_notifications
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Schema: platform_notifications. Knex migration file, not raw SQL.

userStories.title: Notification repository
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a repository layer for DB access so that services and routes never write raw SQL outside the repository.
acceptanceCriteria: src/repositories/notifications.repo.ts exports NotificationsRepo class
insert(data: { id, channel, title, body?, payload?, expires_at }): Promise<{ id, seq }> — inserts into notifications

findHistory({ channels, userId, unread?, before?, limit }): Promise<{ rows: NotificationRow[], nextCursor: string | null, totalUnread: number }> — LEFT JOINs notification_reads, applies unread filter, cursor pagination on (created_at DESC, id DESC), totalUnread count ignores limit/before
findMissed({ channels, afterSeq, limit }): Promise<{ rows: NotificationRow[], truncated: boolean }> — returns rows with seq > afterSeq ordered by seq ASC, capped at limit (200)
markRead(userId: string, notificationId: string): Promise<boolean> — upsert into notification_reads; returns false if notification not found or expired
markAllRead(userId: string, channels: string[]): Promise<{ ids: string[], count: number }> — bulk upsert for all unread in given channels; returns inserted IDs and count
deleteExpired(): Promise<number> — deletes rows WHERE expires_at < now(), returns deleted count
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Channel validator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need strict channel pattern enforcement so that unknown or malformed channels are rejected fail-closed.
acceptanceCriteria: src/services/channel-validator.ts exports validateChannelPattern(channel: string): boolean
Accepted patterns: location:{id}:{type} (at least 3 segments, non-empty id and type), user:{id}:{type}, global:system
Rejects: unknown:xyz, location: (missing segments), bare strings, empty string, extra colons producing empty segments
Exports validateChannelAccess(channel: string, jwtClaims: { sub: string, locations?: string[] }): boolean — for location:* checks locations array, for user:* checks sub, for global:* returns true
Exports ALL_CHANNEL_PREFIXES constant listing accepted prefixes
Unit tests in test/unit/channel-validator.test.ts covering all accept/reject cases from §9 of spec
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Rate limiter service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a per-channel sliding-window rate limiter in Redis so that a runaway caller cannot flood a channel.
acceptanceCriteria: src/services/rate-limiter.ts exports RateLimiter class taking an ioredis client
checkAndIncrement(channel: string): Promise<{ allowed: boolean, retryAfterSeconds?: number }> — uses Redis INCR + EXPIRE on key ratelimit:channel:{channel}, 100 req/60s window
Uses a Lua script (or pipeline) to atomically increment and set TTL only on first call in window
Returns retryAfterSeconds (remaining TTL) when limit exceeded
Unit tests in test/unit/rate-limiter.test.ts: first 100 calls allowed, 101st returns allowed: false, after window expires counter resets (mock Redis or real Redis in unit test with setup/teardown)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Redis key: ratelimit:channel:{channel}. Sliding window via INCR + TTL.

userStories.title: Publisher service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a publisher service that writes to the DB and fans out to Redis so that notifications are durable and delivered in real time.
acceptanceCriteria: src/services/publisher.ts exports Publisher class

publish({ channel, title, body?, payload? }): Promise<{ notification_id: string }> — inserts DB row with expires_at = now()+7d, attempts Redis PUBLISH to notif:channel:{channel} with JSON payload {notification_id, seq, channel, title, body, payload, created_at}
On Redis PUBLISH success: returns { notification_id }
On Redis PUBLISH failure: enqueues BullMQ job in publish-retry queue with { notification_id, channel, seq, title, body, payload, created_at }, then returns { notification_id }
DB insert always commits before Redis attempt; Redis failure never causes 5xx
Unit tests in test/unit/publisher.test.ts: DB insert called with correct fields, Redis PUBLISH called with namespaced key, on Redis throw BullMQ job enqueued, notification_id returned in both paths
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Redis pub/sub key: notif:channel:{channel}. BullMQ queue name: publish-retry.

userStories.title: SSE Manager
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an SSE Manager that tracks live connections and routes Redis pub/sub messages to the correct browser clients.
acceptanceCriteria: src/services/sse-manager.ts exports SseManager class
SseConnection type: { id: string (UUID), userId: string, channels: Set<string>, reply: FastifyReply, connectionId: string }
register(conn: SseConnection): void — adds to channelMap (channel → Set<SseConnection>) and userMap (userId → Set<SseConnection>)
deregister(conn: SseConnection): void — removes from both maps
Per-user connection limit: when the 11th connection arrives for a userId, sends event: connection-limit to oldest connection then closes it before registering the new one

Subscribes to Redis PSUBSCRIBE 'notif:*' on init; on message: routes notif:channel:* to channelMap fan-out writing event: notification SSE; routes notif:user:*:reads to userMap fan-out writing event: read or event: read-all, excluding originating_connection_id
sendKeepalive(conn): writes ': keepalive\n\n' comment
Unit tests in test/unit/sse-manager.test.ts covering all scenarios listed in §9 of spec (register, deregister, fan-out, read-sync, connection limit, originating connection exclusion)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In-memory maps only. Redis PSUBSCRIBE pattern: 'notif:*'. SSE events use \n\n line endings.

userStories.title: POST /notifications/publish route
userStories.description: As a product service, I want to publish a notification via REST so that browser clients can be notified in real time.
acceptanceCriteria: src/routes/publish.ts registers POST /notifications/publish on the Fastify instance
Validates Authorization: Bearer <token> as HMAC-signed JWT (secret from config); returns 401 if missing, 403 if invalid

Validates request body with TypeBox: channel (required string), title (required string), body (optional string), payload (optional object, max 4096 UTF-8 bytes); returns 400 with descriptive error on validation failure
Validates channel against channel-validator; returns 400 { error: 'invalid_channel_pattern' } if unrecognized
Checks rate limiter; returns 429 with Retry-After header if exceeded
Calls Publisher.publish(); returns 201 { notification_id }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Service-to-service JWT validated with jose (HMAC HS256). Not exposed publicly — internal VPC only.

userStories.title: GET /notifications/stream SSE route
userStories.description: As a browser client, I want to open an SSE stream so that I receive real-time notification events.
acceptanceCriteria: src/routes/stream.ts registers GET /notifications/stream on the Fastify instance
Validates Authorization: Bearer <token> (user JWT); extracts userId and location claims; returns 401/403 on failure
Parses ?channels= comma-separated list; returns 400 if any channel pattern invalid; returns 403 if any channel fails access control
Sets response headers: Content-Type: text/event-stream, Cache-Control: no-cache, Connection: keep-alive, X-Connection-ID: <new UUID>
Handles per-user connection limit via SseManager (evicts oldest, sends connection-limit event)

On connect: if Last-Event-ID header present, queries missed notifications (seq > last_event_id, capped at 200); if truncated sends replay-truncated event first, then replays missed events as event: notification
Registers connection in SseManager; starts 30s keepalive interval
On client disconnect: deregisters from SseManager, clears keepalive interval
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Fastify raw reply (reply.raw) to write SSE. fetch-event-source on client side sends Authorization header — server only does standard SSE.

userStories.title: GET /notifications history endpoint
userStories.description: As a browser client, I want to fetch paginated notification history so that I can display the notification list and unread badge count.
acceptanceCriteria: src/routes/notifications.ts registers GET /notifications
Query params: channels (required, comma-separated), unread (optional boolean), limit (optional, default 50, max 100), before (optional cursor string)
Validates channel patterns; returns 400 on invalid; validates access control; returns 403 on failure
Calls NotificationsRepo.findHistory(); returns { notifications: [...], next_cursor: string | null }
Response includes X-Total-Count header with total unread count across requested channels for this user (ignoring pagination)
Each notification row includes: notification_id, channel, title, body, payload, read (boolean), created_at
Expired notifications not returned
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: User JWT validated for all /notifications routes. X-Total-Count: 0 when unread filter not applied.

userStories.title: POST mark-read endpoints
userStories.description: As a browser client, I want to mark notifications as read so that read state syncs across all my open tabs.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /notifications/:id/read registered in src/routes/notifications.ts
Reads X-Connection-ID header from request
Calls NotificationsRepo.markRead(userId, notificationId); returns 404 if notification not found or expired
On success: Redis PUBLISH to notif:user:{userId}:reads with { notification_id, originating_connection_id }; returns 200 {}
POST /notifications/read-all registered in src/routes/notifications.ts
Body: { channels: string[] }; validates channel patterns and access control; returns 400/403 on failure
Calls NotificationsRepo.markAllRead(userId, channels); Redis PUBLISH single message { notification_ids: [...], originating_connection_id } to notif:user:{userId}:reads
Returns 200 { marked: <count> }
Both endpoints require user JWT; idempotent (upsert on conflict)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Redis PUBLISH key: notif:user:{userId}:reads. SseManager excludes originating connection based on originating_connection_id.

userStories.title: BullMQ publish-retry worker
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a BullMQ worker that retries Redis PUBLISH after failure so that no notification is permanently lost if Redis is temporarily unavailable.
acceptanceCriteria: src/queue/publish-retry.worker.ts creates a BullMQ Worker on queue publish-retry
Job data: { notification_id, channel, seq, title, body, payload, created_at }
Worker attempts Redis PUBLISH to notif:channel:{channel} with the stored payload
On success: job marked complete
Retry config: 3 attempts, delays 1000ms / 5000ms / 30000ms (exponential backoff via BullMQ attempts + backoff settings)
After 3 failures job is moved to failed queue (BullMQ default)
Worker is started in src/index.ts alongside the Fastify server
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: BullMQ queue name: publish-retry. Redis client shared with pub/sub (separate ioredis connection for blocking operations).

userStories.title: BullMQ cleanup worker
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a daily cron job that deletes expired notifications so that the database does not grow unbounded.
acceptanceCriteria: src/queue/cleanup.worker.ts creates a BullMQ repeatable job on queue notification-cleanup with cron '0 2 * * *' (2:00 AM UTC)
Worker calls NotificationsRepo.deleteExpired(); logs count of deleted rows
BullMQ deduplication via job key ensures only one instance runs the job at a time across all ECS tasks
Worker is started in src/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: ON DELETE CASCADE on notification_reads ensures read receipts are cleaned up automatically.

userStories.title: Integration tests — publish and SSE stream
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests against real Postgres and Redis so that the end-to-end publish→stream flow is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: test/integration/publish-stream.test.ts: publish to channel X → SSE client subscribed to X receives event: notification with correct payload; publish to channel X → SSE client subscribed to Y receives nothing

test/integration/read-sync.test.ts: mark read → notification_reads row inserted; second SSE connection for same user receives event: read; X-Connection-ID suppresses echo on originating connection; calling mark-read twice is idempotent (no error); mark read on expired/non-existent notification returns 404

test/integration/read-all.test.ts: POST /read-all marks all unread in channels; single event: read-all pushed to other connections with full notification_ids array; response includes correct marked count

test/integration/history.test.ts: GET /notifications returns history with correct read: true/false per item; unread=true filter works; X-Total-Count matches actual unread count; pagination cursor advances correctly; expired notifications not returned

test/integration/replay.test.ts: reconnect with Last-Event-ID replays missed notifications without duplicates; reconnect with > 200 missed sends replay-truncated first with correct replayed/first_seq, then 200 events
test/integration/auth.test.ts: subscribe to unauthorized channel returns 403; subscribe with unknown pattern returns 400; publish with invalid service JWT returns 401/403
test/integration/rate-limit.test.ts: 101st publish within 60s returns 429 with Retry-After header
test/integration/redis-failure.test.ts: Redis unavailable during publish → DB write succeeds, 201 returned, BullMQ retry job created → after Redis recovers worker delivers to subscribed clients
test/integration/cleanup.test.ts: cleanup worker deletes rows where expires_at < now(); notification_reads cascade-deleted
Tests use real Postgres (platform_notifications schema) and real Redis (test instance); test helpers create/teardown schema per test suite
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use @ortho/testing helpers if available, otherwise local test fixtures. Each test file runs migrations before suite and truncates tables between tests.
